title: Clipse & Pharrell On 'Let God Sort Em Out', Grammys & The State of Rap
text: Billboard interview with Clipse and Pharrell about their Let God Sort Em Out album and Grammy aspirations. Album Success: Clipse's reunion album debuted at No. 4 on the Billboard 200, marking their highest chart position since 2002's Lord Willin'. The project has generated significant year-end album conversations and critical acclaim. Breaking Age Barriers in Rap: Pusha T emphasized that hip-hop shouldn't have an age ceiling, noting: I've personally always wanted to make it my business to crack that ceiling. Pharrell referenced Martin Scorsese's The Irishman, stating You don't stop being great. Creative Philosophy: The trio described their approach as creating with emotion rather than just sounds. Pharrell explained: we were painting with feelings...The music is the drug. Grammy Aspirations: Despite decades in the industry, Clipse has never won a Grammy. Malice stated plainly: You don't play the game just to get a participation trophy. Pharrell emphasized the win would honor their parents. Upcoming Plans: The group recently performed at the Vatican and will make their Coachella debut in April 2026.
